compliance screening is live in ampersend today.

once enabled, every agent transaction running through the ampersend sdk passes through a policy engine before settlement. counterparty wallets get evaluated by @trmlabs for sanctions exposure, ransomware links, and other high-risk activity in milliseconds. sellers configure the risk threshold inside the dashboard and ampersend enforces it on every payment the agent attempts.

blocked transactions return 403 forbidden and never settle. clean wallets continue through at the full speed of stablecoin rails. every decision lands in the audit log with risk score, category breakdown, and timestamp.

the controls layer for autonomous payments is shipping. start screening today at https://t.co/5NgHP3Gytb.

We are continuing to investigate yesterday’s incident and are working through a full security review with internal and external teams. As a precautionary measure, certain Bankr functionality will remain temporarily disabled while we complete that process. Services will be re-enabled once we are confident the platform is secure. We appreciate everyone’s patience and will continue to share updates as appropriate.\n\nWe are also coordinating with law enforcement, including the FBI, as well as relevant third parties and counterparties in efforts to identify, freeze, and recover assets where possible and support potential enforcement actions against the responsible actors.

I asked an agent to create a oneliner pitch/explainer for each of the top 100 digital assets on @coingecko

1. Bitcoin BTC sovereign digital money with the strongest brand, deepest liquidity, and hardest “digital gold” narrative.
2. Ethereum ETH dominant smart-contract settlement layer powering DeFi, stablecoins, NFTs, L2s, and most crypto experimentation.
3. Tether USDT the largest dollar stablecoin, heavily used for global trading, payments, and offshore dollar liquidity.
4. BNB BNB Binance-linked exchange and chain token used for fees, DeFi, launches, and ecosystem incentives.
5. XRP XRP fast payments-focused asset built around cross-border settlement, liquidity, and Ripple’s institutional network.
6. USDC USDC regulated dollar stablecoin from Circle, widely used for compliant payments, DeFi, and tokenized finance.
7. Solana SOL high-throughput L1 optimized for cheap, fast consumer apps, trading, DePIN, and memecoin activity.
8. TRON TRX payments-heavy chain best known for massive USDT transfer volume and low-cost stablecoin rails.
9. Figure HELOC FIGR_HELOC tokenized home-equity-loan exposure linked to Figure’s on-chain credit/securitization platform.
10. Dogecoin DOGE original memecoin with unmatched cultural staying power and retail-driven liquidity.
11. WhiteBIT Coin WBT exchange token for the WhiteBIT ecosystem, tied to trading perks, fees, and platform utility.
12. Hyperliquid HYPE token for a fast-growing on-chain perpetuals exchange and high-performance trading ecosystem.
13. USDS USDS Sky/Maker ecosystem dollar stablecoin, evolved from DAI’s collateralized stablecoin model.
14. Zcash ZEC privacy-focused digital cash using zero-knowledge proofs for shielded transactions.
15. Cardano ADA research-led smart-contract L1 focused on formal methods, staking, governance, and long-horizon infrastructure.
16. LEO Token LEO Bitfinex/iFinex exchange token with fee discounts and buyback-style token economics.
17. Bitcoin Cash BCH Bitcoin fork focused on low-fee peer-to-peer payments and larger-block transaction throughput.
18. Monero XMR privacy-first money using default obfuscation to hide sender, receiver, and amount.
19. Chainlink LINK oracle and cross-chain infrastructure network connecting smart contracts to real-world data and systems.
20. Canton CC privacy-enabled institutional blockchain network for tokenized assets and regulated financial markets.
21. Toncoin TON Telegram-adjacent L1 ecosystem aimed at consumer crypto, payments, mini-apps, and messaging-native finance.
22. Stellar XLM fast, low-cost payments network optimized for stablecoins, remittances, tokenized assets, and financial inclusion.
23. USD1 USD1 dollar stablecoin designed to track USD for payments, trading, and liquidity.
24. Ethena USDe USDE synthetic dollar built from crypto collateral and hedging strategies rather than traditional bank deposits.
25. MemeCore M meme-focused chain/ecosystem trying to turn memecoin culture into a dedicated on-chain economy.
26. Dai DAI decentralized collateral-backed dollar stablecoin, historically MakerDAO’s flagship product.
27. Sui SUI Move-based high-performance L1 focused on parallel execution, consumer apps, gaming, and digital assets.
28. Litecoin LTC long-running Bitcoin-like payments coin with faster blocks and strong exchange support.
29. Avalanche AVAX scalable smart-contract platform built around subnets, fast finality, DeFi, and custom chains.
30. Hedera HBAR enterprise-focused public ledger using hashgraph consensus for fast, low-cost applications.
31. Rain RAIN stablecoin/payments-linked token category; worth deeper DD because “Rain” branding overlaps multiple crypto projects.
32. PayPal USD PYUSD PayPal’s regulated dollar stablecoin for payments, transfers, and mainstream fintech distribution.
33. Shiba Inu SHIB memecoin ecosystem built around community, branding, DeFi experiments, and speculative liquidity.
34. Cronos CRO Crypto .com ecosystem token used across exchange, chain, payments, and app incentives.
35. Circle USYC USYC tokenized yield-bearing treasury/cash product from Circle’s tokenized asset stack.
36. Global Dollar USDG dollar stablecoin aimed at payments and institutional-grade digital dollar liquidity.
37. Tether Gold XAUT tokenized gold product giving on-chain exposure to physical gold.
38. BlackRock BUIDL BUIDL tokenized institutional money-market fund bringing Treasury yield exposure on-chain.
39. Bittensor TAO decentralized AI network rewarding machine-learning models and compute contributors.
40. Uniswap UNI governance token for the leading decentralized exchange protocol on Ethereum and L2s.
41. Ondo USDY USDY yield-bearing tokenized dollar product backed by short-term Treasuries and bank deposits.
42. NEAR Protocol NEAR scalable L1 with strong focus on usability, sharding, chain abstraction, and AI-adjacent apps.
43. PAX Gold PAXG regulated tokenized gold, with each token representing physical gold exposure.
44. Polkadot DOT interoperable multichain network built around shared security, parachains, and cross-chain messaging.
45. Mantle MNT Ethereum L2 and ecosystem token backed by a large treasury and modular scaling strategy.
46. World Liberty Financial WLFI politically branded DeFi/governance token tied to the World Liberty Financial ecosystem.
47. Ondo ONDO governance/ecosystem token for Ondo’s tokenized real-world asset and Treasury product platform.
48. HTX DAO HTX exchange/DAO token linked to the HTX trading ecosystem and platform incentives.
49. Falcon USD USDF dollar stablecoin designed for crypto trading, collateral, and DeFi liquidity.
50. Aster ASTER DeFi/trading ecosystem token, likely positioned around perpetuals, liquidity, or exchange infrastructure.
51. OKB OKB OKX exchange token used for fees, platform access, and broader exchange ecosystem utility.
52. Ripple USD RLUSD Ripple-issued dollar stablecoin for payments, trading, and institutional settlement.
53. Sky SKY governance token for the Sky ecosystem, the rebranded evolution of MakerDAO.
54. Pi Network PI mobile-mined crypto project with massive user reach but still heavily debated on real utility.
55. Pepe PEPE pure memecoin built around internet culture, liquidity, and speculative attention.
56. USDD USDD TRON-linked overcollateralized/algorithmic-style dollar stablecoin for DeFi and payments.
57. Ethereum Classic ETC original Ethereum chain preserving proof-of-work and pre-DAO-fork history.
58. Bitget Token BGB Bitget exchange token tied to fee benefits, launches, rewards, and platform growth.
59. Internet Computer ICP ambitious web-compute blockchain aiming to host apps, services, and data directly on-chain.
60. Aave AAVE governance token for one of DeFi’s leading lending and borrowing protocols.
61. BFUSD BFUSD Binance-linked stable-value asset used around trading, collateral, or yield-style exchange products.
62. Morpho MORPHO DeFi lending protocol optimizing borrow/lend markets through vaults and peer-to-peer matching.
63. USDtb USDTB Ethena-linked stablecoin backed by tokenized Treasury-style collateral.
64. KuCoin KCS KuCoin exchange token used for fee discounts, rewards, and platform incentives.
65. Quant QNT interoperability token for Overledger, targeting enterprise connectivity between blockchains and legacy systems.
66. Superstate USTB USTB tokenized short-duration US government securities fund for on-chain Treasury exposure.
67. Spiko EU T-Bills EUTBL tokenized European T-bill money-market product for on-chain euro-denominated yield.
68. United Stables U dollar stablecoin-style asset focused on stable-value liquidity and payments.
69. Cosmos Hub ATOM interoperability-focused chain at the center of the Cosmos appchain ecosystem.
70. Algorand ALGO fast, low-cost L1 focused on payments, tokenization, and high-throughput applications.
71. Janus Henderson Anemoy Treasury Fund JTRSY tokenized Treasury fund bringing traditional fixed-income exposure on-chain.
72. Render RENDER decentralized GPU rendering and compute marketplace for creators, AI, and graphics workloads.
73. Blockchain Capital BCAP tokenized venture fund exposure linked to Blockchain Capital’s investment portfolio.
74. POL POL Polygon’s upgraded ecosystem token for staking, gas, and broader Polygon network coordination.
75. Ethena ENA governance token for Ethena’s synthetic dollar and crypto-native yield ecosystem.
76. Kaspa KAS proof-of-work blockDAG network aiming for fast confirmation while preserving mining-based security.
77. NEXO NEXO token for Nexo’s crypto lending, exchange, and wealth platform.
78. Worldcoin WLD identity and distribution token for Worldcoin’s proof-of-personhood network.
79. Venice Token VVV token linked to Venice .ai’s private AI platform and AI-inference access economy.
80. JUST JST TRON-based DeFi governance token for lending, stablecoin, and financial apps.
81. Stable STABLE stablecoin-themed project token; likely tied to stable-value payments or stablecoin infrastructure.
82. Aptos APT Move-based L1 built for high-throughput apps, parallel execution, and consumer-grade UX.
83. Filecoin FIL decentralized storage network paying providers to store and retrieve data.
84. Gate GT gate .io exchange token used for trading discounts, launches, and ecosystem utility.
85. Flare FLR data-focused L1 bringing decentralized price feeds and cross-chain data to smart contracts.
86. XDC Network XDC enterprise and trade-finance-focused blockchain targeting tokenization, payments, and real-world settlement.
87. Arbitrum ARB governance token for the leading Ethereum optimistic rollup ecosystem.
88. Jupiter JUP Solana liquidity aggregator and trading platform token powering swaps, perps, and governance.
89. DeXe DEXE DAO and governance infrastructure token for decentralized treasury and organization management.
90. Beldex BDX privacy-focused chain/ecosystem combining private payments with privacy-preserving apps.
91. Pump .fun PUMP token tied to Solana’s viral memecoin launchpad and creator/speculation economy.
92. Dash DASH payments-focused Bitcoin fork with instant-send features, masternodes, and privacy legacy.
93. GHO GHO Aave-native decentralized stablecoin minted against collateral in the Aave ecosystem.
94. VeChain VET enterprise supply-chain and real-world asset tracking blockchain.
95. Provenance HASH token for Provenance Blockchain, focused on financial services, asset tokenization, and on-chain capital markets.
96. Usual USD USD0 stablecoin backed by real-world assets and designed around shared protocol value capture.
97. Pudgy Penguins PENGU NFT brand/community token expanding Pudgy Penguins into broader consumer crypto culture.
98. OUSG OUSG Ondo tokenized short-term US Treasury product for institutional-style on-chain yield.
99. Bonk BONK Solana-native memecoin that became a major liquidity and community asset.
100. Kite KITE agentic-payments-focused L1 for AI agents, stablecoin settlement, and x402-style autonomous transactions.
